Serving All of Bastrop County, TX

Bastrop County Stucco Services

Bastrop County homes face a distinct set of exterior challenges — from wildfire exposure and severe thunderstorms to the everyday toll of Texas heat on aging stucco. Star Stucco of Austin has helped more than 200 Bastrop County property owners restore and protect their homes with professional stucco repair and installation services designed for this environment.

Get a Free Stucco Estimate Call Now: (512) 706-9699

Stucco Solutions for Bastrop County Properties

Bastrop County stretches along the Colorado River east of Austin, blending post-oak woodlands, sandy soils, and open farmland into a landscape that attracts both rural and suburban homeowners. The area experienced devastating wildfires in 2011 and again in following years, leaving many properties with heat-damaged or entirely destroyed stucco exteriors. Rebuilding and repairing those finishes remains an active part of our work in the county.

Beyond fire damage, the sandy loam soils prevalent across Bastrop County drain quickly but offer inconsistent support for foundations. Homes settle unevenly over time, and that movement cracks stucco walls in predictable patterns. Add heavy spring rains, hailstorms, and summer temperatures that routinely exceed 100 degrees, and you have an environment that tests every exterior finish to its limits.

Whether your home is in a well-established neighborhood in Bastrop, a newer subdivision near Elgin, or a country property off a farm road near Smithville, we bring the tools and know-how to address your stucco needs. Our team has worked across the full range of conditions this county presents, and we build every repair and installation to handle what comes next.

Stucco Services We Offer in Bastrop County

Professional stucco and plaster solutions for homes and businesses throughout Bastrop County.

Stucco Repair

Storm damage, foundation settlement, and heat exposure are the primary drivers of stucco failures in Bastrop County. We repair every type of damage — from surface cracks to deep structural issues — using methods that account for the sandy soils and weather patterns unique to this area.

Learn More

Stucco Installation

New homes and rebuild projects across Bastrop County benefit from stucco exteriors that resist fire, weather, and pest intrusion. We install complete stucco systems from the moisture barrier outward, creating a durable shell suited to the local climate.

Learn More

Stucco Crack Repair

Sandy loam soils in Bastrop County compact and shift unpredictably, generating cracks that run through stucco along stress lines. We cut out damaged sections, reinforce the substrate with mesh where needed, and apply repair coats that flex with future ground movement.

Learn More

Stucco Water Damage Repair

Heavy spring rains saturate Bastrop County soils and push water against foundation walls and lower stucco courses. We strip away water-damaged material, dry the underlying structure, treat for mold, and rebuild with drainage details that redirect future moisture.

Learn More

Exterior Plaster

Rural homes and ranch properties throughout Bastrop County often feature traditional plaster finishes that have weathered decades of use. We restore these exteriors with compatible plaster mixes and hand-applied techniques that maintain the rustic character of the original work.

Learn More

EIFS Repair & Installation

EIFS offers Bastrop County homeowners improved insulation and a continuous exterior finish with no joints to leak. We install synthetic stucco with proper drainage channels and repair existing systems that have suffered impact or moisture damage from local storms.

Learn More

Commercial Stucco

Businesses in downtown Bastrop, along Highway 71, and throughout the Elgin commercial district rely on stucco for a polished, low-maintenance exterior. We serve commercial clients with efficient scheduling and finishes rated for high-traffic environments.

Learn More

Hands-on experience repairing wildfire and severe weather damage on Bastrop County properties

Understanding of local sandy soils and how they affect foundation movement and stucco performance

Thorough documentation of damage for homeowners filing insurance claims after storms or fires

Honest assessments that prioritize necessary work over unnecessary upselling

Local Expertise

We understand Bastrop County soil types, weather patterns, and building codes for every community.

Fully Licensed & Insured

Licensed Texas contractor with full liability and workers' compensation coverage for every Bastrop County project.

Fast Response

Prompt estimates and scheduling for all Bastrop County communities. Most projects begin within 1–2 weeks.

Common Stucco Problems We Fix in Bastrop County

Bastrop County properties deal with a combination of fire risk, unpredictable weather, and shifting sandy soils that creates a unique set of stucco problems. Here is what we see and fix most often.

Cracks

Sandy loam soils across Bastrop County provide inconsistent foundation support, especially during cycles of drought and heavy rain. This uneven settling produces cracks in stucco that often concentrate around window and door openings where stress is highest.

Bubbling or soft stucco

Poor drainage around many Bastrop County homes allows water to pool at the base of walls and wick upward into the stucco. The resulting soft, bubbled areas are a sign that moisture has been present for an extended period and the base coat needs replacement.

Water stains

Red-brown stains on stucco walls in Bastrop County typically come from iron-rich sandy soil splashing onto surfaces during rainstorms. While these stains are primarily cosmetic, they can also indicate that water is sitting against the wall longer than it should.

Window and door leaks

Wind-driven rain during Bastrop County thunderstorms forces water into gaps around window and door frames. Many homes in the area lack integrated pan flashings beneath windows, making these openings the most common source of interior water damage.

Failing patches

Quick fixes applied with hardware-store patching compounds are common on rural Bastrop County properties where professional repair was not readily available. These patches often crack or pop off within a year because they lack proper adhesion to the existing substrate.

Separation around trim

Extreme heat in Bastrop County causes trim materials to expand away from stucco during summer afternoons, then contract at night. This daily cycling opens and closes gaps that eventually become permanent channels for rainwater entry.

Impact damage

Hail, falling branches from the post-oak canopy, and debris carried by straight-line winds all cause impact damage to stucco surfaces in Bastrop County. Properties surrounded by mature trees are particularly vulnerable during storm season.

Faded or stained exterior walls

The combination of relentless sun and smoke exposure from past wildfires has left many Bastrop County stucco surfaces discolored and uneven. Soot deposits bond stubbornly to textured finishes and typically require professional cleaning and re-coating to fully remove.

How We Work in Bastrop County

A clear, straightforward process from first call to finished project.

1

Schedule inspection

Call us or fill out our online request form to book an inspection at your Bastrop County property. We serve the entire county, from the city of Bastrop to outlying rural communities.

2

Review scope

Our technician evaluates your stucco condition, checks for moisture behind the walls, and photographs all areas of concern. We provide a straightforward summary of what we find and what we recommend, prioritized by urgency.

3

Get estimate

You receive a written estimate with clear line items covering every aspect of the proposed work. We are available to discuss options and adjust the plan to align with your budget and timeline.

4

Complete work

Our crew mobilizes with all necessary materials, completes the work according to the agreed scope, and leaves your property clean. We conduct a final inspection with you to confirm the work meets our shared standards.
